description: Fix lead scoring and enrich API route with referrer, user agent, and proper scoring ranges. The current API route at app/api/lead-capture/route.ts has a critical mismatch: forms send formSource values like 'costsmart-calculator-gate-form' but the scoring switch checks for 'calculator-gate'. Also, the scoring ranges need to match requirements (80-100, 50-70, 30-50, 10-30) and the route needs referrer/user-agent enrichment.
steps: Update app/api/lead-capture/route.ts to fix the formSource switch cases to match actual values sent by forms: 'costsmart-calculator-gate-form', 'costsmart-exit-intent-form', 'costsmart-newsletter-form', 'costsmart-blog-sidebar-form', 'costsmart-bottombar-form'

Update lead scoring ranges to match requirements: calculator-gate = 80-100 (base 80, +10 for name, +10 for calculator context), exit-intent = 50-70 (base 50, +10 for name, +10 for paid UTM), newsletter/blog-sidebar = 30-50 (base 30, +10 for name, +10 for paid UTM), bottombar = 10-30 (base 10, +10 for name, +10 for paid UTM)
Add referrer extraction from request headers (req.headers.get('referer')) to the enriched Zapier payload
Add user-agent extraction from request headers (req.headers.get('user-agent')) to the enriched Zapier payload
Update the LeadCaptureBody interface to include an optional 'referrer' field that client can also pass
Update lib/lead-capture-api.ts to also send document.referrer in the payload
Add a 'lead_tier' field to the Zapier payload that categorizes: 'hot' (score >= 80), 'warm' (score >= 50), 'cool' (score >= 30), 'cold' (score < 30)
Add an 'engagement_path' field recommending automation: hot='immediate_followup', warm='nurture_sequence', cool='weekly_digest', cold='monthly_roundup'
Keep the fire-and-forget pattern for Zapier webhook calls
acceptance_criteria: Running pnpm build succeeds without TypeScript errors
The API route correctly maps all 5 form source identifiers to appropriate scores
Lead scores fall within the specified ranges (80-100, 50-70, 30-50, 10-30)
Zapier payload includes referrer, user_agent, lead_tier, and engagement_path fields
The client-side lib sends document.referrer in the payload
verification: Run 'pnpm build' in /projects/sandbox/cost-smart and confirm it succeeds
Run 'pnpm lint' in /projects/sandbox/cost-smart and confirm no errors
findings: All lint errors (522) are pre-existing in unrelated files (blog posts, scripts, etc.). Verified by running lint on base state which shows same count. The two modified files pass lint cleanly when checked individually. Build passes successfully.
